title: Tenable WAS Remediation Verification Scans
spl:
index=vuln sourcetype="nessus:was" scan_type=verification
| stats count by finding_id, status
| where status!="fixed"
description: Verification scans prove web fixes before production promotion.
value: Verification scans prove web fixes before production promotion.
implementation: Run verification scans after developer fixes. Track reopen rates.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Tenable or Nessus findings on assets with accepted risk, overlapping scans, or already-scheduled remediation; de-duplicate by asset and plugin before chasing every row.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_nessus`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=nessus:was`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Run verification scans after developer fixes. Track reopen rates.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vuln sourcetype="nessus:was" scan_type=verification
| stats count by finding_id, status
| where status!="fixed"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Tenable WAS Remediation Verification Scans** — Verification scans prove web fixes before production promotion.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=nessus:was`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_nessus`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vuln; **sourcetype**: nessus:was. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vuln, sourcetype="nessus:was". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by finding_id, status** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where status!="fixed"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Tenable WAS Remediation Verification Scans** — Verification scans prove web fixes before production promotion.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=nessus:was`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_nessus`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search over a window that includes both normal production traffic and, if you have one, a documented test (vendor sandbox, lab rule hit, or exercise). Compare a few sample events to the vendor’s own console or reports and tune indexes, fields, and thresholds so alerts are actionable before paging.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (open findings), Line chart.
grandmaExplanation: We use Tenable data and alerts to stay ahead of the risks described in "Tenable WAS Remediation Verification Scans". We help the team notice trouble early, while a situation is still small and containable.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
